title: Permanent non-bypassable execution guard: _sanitizeResponse blocks banned phrases before any display path
failing_behavior: Claude's banned availability phrases ('I can't see real-time availability', 'I don't have booking data', etc.) reached the customer even after prompt fixes (RX-022), because _validateResponseQuality only console.warned — it did not actively replace or block the response. Any future prompt regression or model update could reintroduce the phrases with no hard enforcement.
root_cause: The only enforcement was prompt instructions + a console.warn in _validateResponseQuality. Neither actually blocked Claude's text from reaching the customer. The enforcement layer had no teeth — it was advisory only.
fix_summary: Added _sanitizeResponse(biz, result) — a non-bypassable execution guard called in send() as the FIRST operation after _handleMessage resolves, before earlyCheckReady, before escalation handling, before _appendMessage. Uses 16 regex patterns (broader than QV warn list) to detect banned phrases. On match: (1) replaces result.text with a context-aware safe response (asks next missing booking field, or 'What can I help with?'); (2) clears result.escalationType to prevent a bad escalation from the sanitized response; (3) corrects _aiHistory to overwrite Claude's bad text with the safe replacement — so future Claude turns don't see or build on the bad response; (4) logs [GUARD] warning with original text fragment for debugging. The function is architecturally upstream of every display path — it cannot be bypassed regardless of what Claude generates.
guard_properties: Fires on 100% of Claude responses (not just booking turns)
_aiHistory corrected before _saveHistory — Claude never sees the bad text
escalationType cleared — prevents escalation on sanitized response
context-aware replacement — asks next missing booking field in correct language
16 regex patterns cover paraphrases and variations of banned phrases

conversation.content: [GUARD INTERCEPTS THIS] I can't see real-time availability, but Helen works until 7 PM. → replaced with: 'What day and time would you like with Helen?'

bad_examples: I can't see real-time availability, but Helen works until 7 PM.
I don't have access to real-time booking data.
I cannot verify if that slot is open.
The system will check that availability.
My access is limited to schedule data.
I only have schedule data, not booking data.
notes: This is a redundant enforcement layer on top of the prompt fix (RX-022). Even if Claude ignores the prompt or a model update regresses the behavior, _sanitizeResponse will intercept. The [GUARD] log makes regressions visible immediately. Does not affect valid responses — only fires on exact pattern matches of known-bad phrases.
